idea.title: Tiny Model Dodger
idea.hook: A sub-1MB neural net regenerates the obstacle field every 8 ticks based on how you played the last 8 — survive against a bullet curtain that learns your dodge patterns.
idea.hook_ru: Маленькая модель (меньше 1 МБ) пересчитывает поле препятствий каждые 8 тиков на основе твоих предыдущих 8 — выживай против пуль, которые учат твой стиль уклонения.
idea.prototype: Single-screen vertical dodger, ship at bottom, falling hazards from top. A tiny Markov chain (7x7 transition table baked into JS, ~200 bytes of state) tracks player position bins over the last 8 ticks and biases next hazard spawn columns toward the bin you visit most. Player has 3 hearts. Score = ticks survived. The 'AI' adapts within a run, resets per run, and ships visibly in the cover (animated weight matrix corner overlay). Pure arrow keys / touch swipe. Daily seed = initial transition table prior.
idea.why_novel: First arcade where the difficulty model is literally on-screen as a live weight matrix you can read while playing — the 'ML cartridge' is the marketing angle. Tim's pre-approved IDEAS.md bullet (2026-05-14): the goal is a data point on whether sub-1MB ML in hypercasual ships at all, not whether the game is good. Dodger is the smallest sensible genre.
idea.non_timer_pressure: 3 hearts (lives budget) + adaptive Markov pressure that biases hazard spawns toward the player's most-visited column every 8 ticks. No countdown timer anywhere in the game.

sources.title: Tiny-model PCG one-off (Tim's IDEAS.md bullet, 2026-05-14)
sources.snippet: Pre-approved bullet: ship a one-off where a sub-1MB ML model drives PCG inside an HTML5 game. Data-point goal: does sub-1MB ML in hypercasual ship at all?

sources.title: Tiny Model Dodger (digest entry #3)
sources.snippet: Entry #3 — A sub-1MB neural net regenerates the obstacle field every 8 ticks based on how you played the last 8 — survive against a bullet curtain that learns your dodge patterns.

sources.title: Engine designed for AI agents — incremental/casual genre focus
sources.snippet: Делаю движок для 3D-игр на веб — движок рассчитан на то, что игры в нём делает не человек, а ИИ-агенты. Жанровый фокус — инкрементальные/казуальные.

sources.title: Blockworld devlog — Yandex public test prep
sources.snippet: Девлог по Blockworld — готовимся к публичному тестированию на Yandex Games. Cheap-model + agent-cost framing for tooling signal.

synthesizer_brief: Single-screen vertical dodger. Ship locked to a horizontal track near the bottom, hazards fall from the top in one of 7 lane bins. A 7x7 Markov transition table (baked from a daily-seeded prior, updated online with player's observed lane transitions) is used to bias the next hazard spawn distribution toward the player's most-visited lane. Resamples every 8 ticks. Visible live HUD overlay (7x7 heatmap + aim-distribution strip) sells the 'ML cartridge' angle. 3 hearts, endless score-attack, best persists.

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap weak fault lines on a frozen lake to crack ice into chunks that drift apart and ferry stranded penguins to shore before the floe count runs out.
alternatives.why_novel: Fault-line cracking + drift physics with a floe-count budget pressure

alternatives.title: Cargo Chain Junction
alternatives.hook: Route boxcars through a forking rail junction by flipping switches to match the destination depot before queue overflows.
alternatives.why_novel: Real-time route-matching with depot-color queue pressure

alternatives.title: Hook Test Lab
alternatives.hook: A/B test two mini-game variants in front of a simulated player panel and pick the cover/title that wins the most retention.

alternatives.title: Shader Polish Sprint
alternatives.hook: Spend a 10-point polish budget on screen-space VFX (bloom, chromatic, vignette, scanlines) to push a flat prototype's perceived quality.
alternatives.why_novel: VFX-budget-as-puzzle on a fixed reference scene
